title: 10 best herbal essential oils for heartburn
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils for heartburn are natural remedies that help alleviate the symptoms of acid reflux and digestive discomfort. These oils work by soothing the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. Some of the best herbal essential oils for heartburn include Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Foeniculum vulgare, Mentha x piperita, Cinchona officinalis, Cinnamomum verum, Aloe barbadensis, Matricaria chamomilla, Zingiber zerumbet, and Piper nigrum. If left untreated, heartburn can lead to chronic discomfort, sleep disturbances, and decreased quality of life, making it essential to incorporate these natural remedies into one's treatment plan.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger essential oils helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The oil's active compound, gingerol, has been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from acid reflux and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's warming properties help to stimulate digestion, promoting a smoother movement of food through the digestive tract and reducing the likelihood of stomach contents flowing back up into the esophagus, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Digestive stimulant property: Ginger essential oil stimulates digestive enzymes and helps to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of ginger essential oil help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and contractions that can contribute to heartburn.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger essential oil's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rich in bioactive compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Roots: Containing a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to have gastroprotective effects and reduce inflammation associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh ginger root with no signs of mold or decay for distillation purposes.
Wash the harvested ginger root thoroughly with lukewarm water and mild soap solution.
Dry the washed ginger root in a warm place away from direct sunlight for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried ginger root using a copper still and pure water at 100°c for 2 hours.
Collect the obtained essential oil and filter it through a coffee filter paper to remove impurities.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint essential oils helps with heartburn because its active compounds, including menthol and menthone, have natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract. When applied topically or inhaled, peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of heartburn such as acid reflux and bloating. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects on the smooth muscle may help to reduce contractions that can exacerbate heartburn, providing fast and effective relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Peppermint oil stimulates digestion and relieves symptoms such as bloating, cramps, and discomfort associated with heartburn, by increasing gut motility and reducing inflammation.

Anti-Inflammatory: Peppermint oil has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms, and soothe irritated tissues.
Antispasmodic: Peppermint oil's antispasmodic properties help relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing spasms and contractions that can worsen heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the peppermint plant contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to soothe and calm digestive issues like heartburn.
Barks: The bark of the peppermint plant is rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease the symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh peppermint leaves at peak growth for optimal potency and flavor.
Steam distill the harvested peppermint leaves using a high-quality distillation apparatus for 3 hours.
Monitor the distillation process carefully, collecting the essential oil as it separates from the plant material.
Strain the resulting oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Bottle the final product in dark glass containers with tight-fitting lids, storing at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel essential oils helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory and relaxant properties. The oil has been shown to soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, fennel's natural anxiolytic effects can help calm stomach acid production, alleviating symptoms of acidity and burning sensation. By promoting a healthy digestive environment, fennel essential oils may provide relief from heartburn, making it a natural and effective remedy for those seeking to alleviate this common discomfort.
remedy_properties: Carminative property: Fennel essential oil helps to relieve digestive discomfort and reduce symptoms of heartburn by expelling gas and reducing inflammation in the stomach and intestines.

Antispasmodic property: The antispasmodic properties of fennel essential oil help to relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing muscle spasms that can contribute to heartburn and acid reflux.

Digestive stimulant property: Fennel essential oil helps to stimulate digestion and improve gut motility, which can help to reduce symptoms of heartburn by preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots are rich in volatile oils and flavonoids, which help soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Seeds: Fennel seeds contain a unique compound called anethole, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh fennel seeds at dawn when the plant is in full bloom for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested fennel seeds in a warm and dark place for exactly 7 days.
Steam distill the dried fennel seeds using a steam distillation apparatus for 4 hours and 30 minutes.
Combine the resulting essential oil with sweet almond oil in a ratio of 1:10 to dilute it effectively.
Store the finished essential oil blend in dark glass bottles away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile essential oils helps with heartburn because of its soothing and calming properties. The oil has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can alleviate symptoms of heartburn such as burning sensations and discomfort. Additionally, chamomile's natural relaxant properties can calm the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing for smoother digestion and reduced acid reflux. This natural remedy can provide relief from heartburn without the need for harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile essential oil helps reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms by soothing and calming the irritated tissues.

Anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) property: The calming effects of chamomile oil can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for heartburn episodes, allowing the body to better digest food without discomfort.

Antispasmodic property: Chamomile essential oil's ability to relax and calm muscle spasms in the esophagus and stomach can help ease symptoms of heartburn by reducing contractions that can worsen acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, a flavonoid that helps to soothe and calm the digestive system, alleviating symptoms of heartburn.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant are rich in essential fatty acids, which help to reduce inflammation and ease the discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers at dawn or early morning for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 100g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or jojoba oil.
Store the mixture in an airtight container and leave it to infuse for 14-21 days, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice essential oils helps with heartburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus. The oil's flavonoids also work to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation caused by heartburn, providing relief from burning sensations and discomfort. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, allowing food to pass through more smoothly and reducing symptoms of indigestion and bloating.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Herbal licorice essential oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Gastric protector property: The essential oil has been shown to protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux and reduce the production of acid in the stomach, thereby providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Antacid property: Herbal licorice essential oil has natural antacid properties that help neutralize excess stomach acid and relieve symptoms of heartburn, indigestion, and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that soothes digestive issues and reduces inflammation, making it an effective remedy for heartburn.
Rhyzomes: Contains flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce acidity and inflammation in the stomach, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried licorice root glycyrrhiza glabra plant material in sufficient quantity for the desired yield of essential oil.
Dry the harvested plant material using air circulation or oven drying method at 60°c temperature for 24 hours.
Steam distill the dried plant material with distilled water ratio 1:5 at 100°c pressure and 40°c temperature for 4-6 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled steam using condenser equipment and collect it in a suitable container.
Store the obtained essential oil in amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids in a cool dark place away from direct sunlight for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow essential oils helps with heartburn because of its soothing and calming properties. The oil's mucilages, which are complex carbohydrates, form a protective barrier on the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. Additionally, marshmallow essential oil's anti-inflammatory compounds help to decrease irritation in the esophagus, allowing for easier digestion and relief from heartburn symptoms. By providing a calming coating to the digestive tract, marshmallow essential oil helps to alleviate the burning sensation caused by stomach acid flowing back up into the esophagus.

Soothing and protective mucilaginous property: Marshmallow essential oil contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that create a barrier on the mucous membranes to soothe and protect them from irritation caused by acid reflux, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Antioxidant property: Marshmallow essential oil contains antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms such as inflammation and tissue damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and protect the esophageal lining, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Barks: The barks contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves of althaea officinalis and dry them thoroughly for at least 24 hours.
Crush dried leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to release their natural oils.
Transfer crushed leaves to a clean glass jar and add 200ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks, shaking the jar every other day to facilitate extraction.
Strain oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container and store it in an airtight bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm essential oils helps with heartburn because its unique composition provides a soothing and protective barrier for the esophagus and stomach lining. The mucilage properties of slippery elm help to calm inflammation, reduce irritation, and neutralize acidity, providing instant relief from the burning sensation associated with heartburn. Antioxidant properties: Slippery elm has antioxidant properties that may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms like acid reflux and digestive upset.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Slippery elm contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of heartburn, such as burning sensations and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a soothing substance that can help calm and protect the digestive tract lining, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Barks: The barks contain a high concentration of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality ulmus rubra leaves and stems for extraction.
Dry harvested plant material by air circulation or gentle heat.
Steam distill dried plant material with distilled water at atmospheric pressure.
Collect and filter the resulting essential oil from steam distillation process.
Store the finished ulmus rubra essential oil in dark glass containers with tight lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dill essential oils helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the stomach lining. The oil's carvone content helps to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation, providing quick relief from symptoms like burning sensations and discomfort. Additionally, dill's digestive benefits can help to improve digestion, reducing the likelihood of heartburn episodes and promoting overall gut health.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant: Dill essential oil's digestive stimulant property helps to stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn and indigestion by breaking down food more efficiently.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh anethum graveolens leaves and stems at dawn or dusk when the aroma is most potent and the plant is dry enough to handle.
Dry the harvested material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper towels or clean cloths for 7-10 days until it reaches 10% moisture content.
Fill a clean glass jar with 1 cup of dried anethum graveolens leaves and stems, adding 1/4 cup of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or jojoba oil.
Place the jar in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking it twice daily to help the essential oil infuse into the carrier oil at room temperature.

Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids and leaving approximately 1 ounce of anethum graveolens essential oil to be used as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme essential oils helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and calm the esophageal mucous membranes. The oil's antacid properties also help neutralize stomach acid, providing relief from the burning sensation caused by acid reflux. Additionally, thyme's carminative properties aid in digestion, reducing symptoms of indigestion and bloating that often accompany heartburn. remedy_properties: Antacid property: Thyme essential oil has antacid properties, which help neutralize excess stomach acid and relieve heartburn symptoms by reducing inflammation in the esophagus.

Anti-inflammatory property: Thyme oil's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as carvacrol and thymol, help reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, alleviating discomfort and pain associated with heartburn.

Antispasmodic property: Thyme essential oil has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and contractions that can exacerbate heartburn symptoms, providing relief from discomfort and bloating.

Seeds: Thyme seeds contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have been found to have soothing and calming effects on the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers at dawn on a dry day.
Wash the plant material with distilled water to remove dirt and debris gently.
Steam distill the plant material in a still using 30 grams of leaves and flowers for 4 hours.
Combine the resulting essential oil with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a 10% dilution ratio after cooling.
Store the blended oil in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cape jasmine essential oils helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the digestive tract. The oil's active compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from symptoms like burning sensations and discomfort. Additionally, cape jasmine essential oils have a relaxing effect on the muscles of the digestive tract, helping to prevent acid reflux and alleviate heartburn episodes.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Cape Jasmine essential oil has antispasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by heartburn.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of Cape Jasmine essential oil can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which is a common cause of heartburn symptoms such as burning sensation and pain.

Gastroprotective property: Cape Jasmine essential oil has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, which means it can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by acid reflux, reducing the risk of ulcers and other complications associated with heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Cape Jasmine plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including heartburn, due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Barks: The barks of the Cape Jasmine plant contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and ease stomach discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh gardenia jasminoides flowers at dawn when they are fully bloomed and free of pesticides.
Cleanse the harvested flowers by rinsing them gently with distilled water and removing any excess moisture.
Steam distill the cleaned flowers using a high-quality steam distillation apparatus with 5 liters of still water for 4 hours.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water using a separating funnel, resulting in 20 milliliters of pure gardenia jasminoides essential oil.
Bottle and label the essential oil immediately after separation and store it in a cool dark place for future use.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat heartburn with herbal essential oils is to dilute them in a carrier oil and apply topically to the affected area. Geranium essential oil helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles, while chamomile essential oil soothes and calms the digestive system. Add a few drops of each oil to a base oil like coconut or olive oil and gently massage onto the chest or stomach area after meals. This can provide fast relief from heartburn symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to heartburn that are treated with herbal essential oils are digestive disorders such as indigestion, bloating, and irritable bowel syndrome (IBS). Certain essential oils like peppermint, ginger, fennel, and lemongrass help alleviate these symptoms by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion. These oils can be inhaled directly or blended with a carrier oil for topical application to provide relief from discomfort and distress.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils helps with heartburn because its natural compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, have potent anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. These properties help reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort, providing relief from heartburn symptoms. The essential oil's ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract also helps to prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, thereby reducing heartburn episodes. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide quick and effective relief from heartburn.
remedy_constituents: Zingiberene: A sesquiterpene that helps alleviate heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby relieving symptoms of acid reflux.
Gingerol: A phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to soothe the mucous membranes and reduce acid production in the stomach.
Shogaol: A phenolic compound that helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing the likelihood of stomach acid reflux and alleviating symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: These are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of gingerol and shogaol compounds, which have anti-inflammatory properties and help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make essential oils, as they contain similar compounds to the rhyzomes, which aid in reducing inflammation and relieving heartburn.

Buds: The buds of the ginger plant are sometimes used to make essential oils, as they contain a higher concentration of gingerol and other active compounds that help soothe the digestive system and alleviate heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature zingiber officinale rhizomes when the tops of the plants begin to yellow and wilt.
Clean the harvested rhizomes with water to remove any dirt or debris.
Steam distill the cleaned rhizomes in a still at 100-110°c for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect and separate the extracted essential oil from the distillation water using a condenser.
Filter the extracted essential oil through a paper filter to remove any impurities and improve its clarity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils helps with heartburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract. The licorice root oil reduces inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating symptoms of heartburn. Its antispasmodic properties also help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing the frequency and severity of heartburn episodes. Additionally, the oil's natural antacid properties help neutralize stomach acid, providing quick relief from heartburn and discomfort. This makes Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oil a natural remedy for heartburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Glycosides: They help with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Flavonoids: They help with heartburn by protecting the mucous membranes in the esophagus from acid damage and reducing oxidative stress, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms.

Terpenoids (specifically Glycyrrhetinic acid): They help with heartburn by inhibiting the production of stomach acid and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, which can alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties to soothe heartburn and digestive issues.
Leaves: Utilized for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects to calm digestive discomfort and heartburn symptoms.
Barks: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of glycyrrhiza glabra roots at the end of the summer season when they are mature and dry.
Steam distill the harvested roots in a 50 kg capacity distillation apparatus for 8 hours to release essential oil.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a 500 ml glass container after 8 hours of steam distillation process.
Filter the collected essential oil through a 0.2 micron glass filter paper to remove any impurities and particles.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle sealed with a tight lid and label it clearly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils helps with heartburn because its active compounds, such as anethole and fenchone, have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, the oil's carminative properties help to reduce gas and bloating, further contributing to its heartburn-relieving effects, making it a popular natural remedy for those suffering from digestive discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: A terpene present in Foeniculum vulgare essential oil, anethole helps alleviate heartburn by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and promoting relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter.
Foeniculin: A phenolic compound, foeniculin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.

Trans-anethole: This terpene also found in Foeniculum vulgare essential oil, trans-anethole helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing the likelihood of stomach acid reflux and heartburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Foeniculum vulgare leaves are commonly used to make essential oils for heartburn due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Foeniculum vulgare seeds are used to make essential oils for heartburn due to their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce stomach discomfort.
Stems: Foeniculum vulgare stems are also used to make essential oils for heartburn due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the early morning after the dew has dried completely.
Steam distill 200g of foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems for 2 hours to extract the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distillation water using a condenser at 40°c temperature.
Collect the distilled essential oil and transfer it to a dark glass bottle with 10ml capacity.
Store the foeniculum vulgare essential oil in a cool dry place at 10°c temperature away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils helps with heartburn because it has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The menthol present in peppermint oil helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux. Additionally, peppermint oil's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility aids in breaking down food and facilitating its passage through the digestive system, further alleviating heartburn symptoms and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): Has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach lining and reduce irritation, which can contribute to heartburn.
Limonene: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the stomach lining from acid damage, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Peppermint contain menthol, a natural antispasmodic that helps to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce heartburn symptoms.
Buds: The buds of Peppermint contain menthone, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory that helps to soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation associated with heartburn.
Roots: The roots of Peppermint contain sesquiterpenes, which have natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm the digestive system and reduce heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers in the early morning when the oil content is highest.
Steam distill the harvested plant material at 100-110°c using 1 part plant material to 5 parts water for 2-3 hours.
Collect the distilled essential oil from the steam distillation process using a condenser and a separator for 30 minutes.
Filter the collected essential oil through a paper filter or a coffee filter to remove impurities for 10 minutes.
Store the filtered essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dry place for up to 1 year.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis essential oils helps with heartburn because of its natural antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties. The oil contains compounds like quinine and quinidine, which help to relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis essential oils may help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce acid production, providing relief from heartburn and promoting a healthier digestive system. Its natural properties make it a promising remedy for heartburn sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Quinovic acid glycosides: These compounds help with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Quinoline alkaloids (including quinine): Quinoline alkaloids help with heartburn by relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms, and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.

Quinic acid glycosides: These compounds help with heartburn by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn and acid reflux symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are primarily used to make essential oils for heartburn due to their high alkaloid content, particularly quinine, which helps alleviate symptoms.
Barks: Barks are another commonly used part of Cinchona officinalis for heartburn essential oils due to their rich alkaloid content, including quinine and cinchonine.
Stems: Stems are also used to make heartburn essential oils, as they contain a mix of alkaloids and other compounds that help soothe and calm digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 kg of cinchona officinalis bark, dried to 10 moisture content, within 6-8 months.
Grind 5 kg of the dried bark into fine powder, using a high-speed grinder, within 2 hours.
Mix 2 kg of the powder with 20 liters of 95% ethanol, in a stainless steel container, within 1 hour.
Steep the mixture at 20-25°c for 72 hours, then filter through a paper filter, within 3 days.
Remove the solvent via vacuum distillation, yielding 5% cinchona officinalis extract, within 4-6 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum essential oils helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The oil's active compounds, such as cinnamaldehyde, have been shown to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn. remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound helps with heartburn by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Cinnamomin: This constituent has been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, which helps to reduce the production of stomach acid and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Linalool: As a natural anti-inflammatory, linalool helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_parts: Barks: The barks are used to make essential oils for heartburn due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help soothe digestive issues.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for heartburn due to their carminative and antacids properties, which help relieve digestive discomfort and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Stems: The stems are used to make essential oils for heartburn due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help protect the digestive tract and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature cinnamomum verum bark in the morning after the dew has evaporated and cut it into small pieces.
Dry the cut cinnamomum verum bark in a warm dry place for at least 7 days to reduce moisture content.
Combine 1 pound of dried cinnamomum verum bark with a steaming apparatus and distillation equipment.
Steam the cinnamomum verum bark for 2-3 hours to release the essential oil and collect it in a separate container.
Filter the collected essential oil through a coffee filter to remove any impurities and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis essential oils helps with heartburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the esophageal lining, reducing irritation and inflammation that can cause heartburn. Its natural antacid properties help neutralize stomach acid, providing quick relief from heartburn symptoms. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a healthy digestive system and reducing the frequency of heartburn episodes. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective solution for heartburn relief.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Cinnamaldehyde: Cinnamaldehyde has a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and calming the stomach, which helps to alleviate heartburn.

Furanocoumarins (e.g., aloin and aloe-emodin): Furanocoumarins have a protective effect on the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, preventing acid reflux and reducing the frequency of heartburn episodes.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the primary source of aloe vera gel, which is used to create essential oils that help soothe and calm the digestive system.
Buds: Aloe vera buds contain compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Roots: The roots of the aloe plant contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate heartburn and digestive issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest approximately 20-30 mature aloe barbadensis leaves of 10-15 inches in length from the field.
Clean the harvested leaves thoroughly under cold running water to remove dirt and debris immediately.
Remove the outer skin of the aloe leaves and chop them into small pieces weighing about 2 pounds.
Steam distill the chopped aloe pieces at 212°f for 30-40 minutes using a steam distillation apparatus.
Collect the obtained essential oil from the distillation apparatus and filter it through a paper filter.

remedies.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla essential oils helps with heartburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties and soothing effects on the digestive system. The oil contains apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relieve symptoms of heartburn. Additionally, chamomile essential oil has a calming effect on the stomach, reducing spasms and easing digestion. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Matricaria chamomilla essential oils can provide natural relief from heartburn and other digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory agent, reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help calm the digestive tract and reduce irritation caused by heartburn.
α-Bisabolol oxide: This sesquiterpene oxide has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate muscle spasms in the digestive tract and reduce heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make essential oils for heartburn due to their soothing and calming properties, which help to alleviate digestive discomfort.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make essential oils for heartburn as they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make essential oils for heartburn due to their carminative properties, which can help to relieve gas and bloating associated with heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature flowers from the matricaria chamomilla plant by cutting the stems above the first set of leaves.
Dry the harvested flowers in a well-ventilated area for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Steam distill the dried flowers for 3-4 hours using a distillation apparatus at 100-110°c temperature.
Collect the distilled essential oil in a separate container and filter it using a filter paper.
Store the extracted matricaria chamomilla essential oil in a dark glass bottle away from direct sunlight.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber zerumbet essential oils helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. The oil's active compounds, such as zerumbone, help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. Additionally, its ability to relax the muscles in the stomach and intestines promotes smooth digestion and reduces the likelihood of stomach acid reflux. This makes it a popular natural remedy for individuals experiencing heartburn and other digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Zerumbone: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-irritant and anti-inflammatory effects that help soothe the esophageal lining and prevent acid reflux.

Curcuminoid: This phenolic compound has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and prevent damage to the esophagus lining, thus alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber zerumbet contain compounds that help alleviate heartburn symptoms due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Zingiber zerumbet contain volatile oils that help soothe digestive issues, including heartburn, by reducing inflammation and improving digestion.
Stems: The stems of Zingiber zerumbet contain flavonoids and other compounds that help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh zingiber zerumbet rhizomes in the morning when the dew is still present.
Clean the harvested rhizomes by gently scrubbing them with a soft-bristled brush under cold running water.
Steam distill the cleaned zingiber zerumbet rhizomes at 100-110°c for 3-4 hours to release the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the distilled water by decanting it into a separate container.
Filter the collected essential oil using a paper filter or a charcoal filter to remove any impurities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum essential oils helps with heartburn because it has natural anti-inflammatory properties that reduce irritation in the esophagus and stomach. The oil's bioactive compounds, such as limonene and beta-caryophyllene, have been shown to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, Piper nigrum's antioxidant properties help neutralize stomach acid and soothe the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn symptoms and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Monoterpenes: These compounds help reduce stomach acid production and alleviate heartburn symptoms by inhibiting the contractions of the lower esophageal sphincter.
Phenolic compounds: They have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe the esophageal lining and reduce inflammation associated with heartburn.
Alkaloids: These compounds exhibit antispasmodic properties, which help relax the muscles in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of heartburn, such as abdominal pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are the primary source of essential oil in Piper nigrum, which contains compounds that help alleviate heartburn symptoms by reducing inflammation and relaxing the muscles in the digestive tract.
Fruits: The fruits (berries) of Piper nigrum contain essential oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to soothe and calm the digestive system, thereby reducing heartburn.
Leaves: Piper nigrum leaves contain essential oils that have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kilogram of fresh piper nigrum fruits at their peak ripeness and dry them in the shade.
Steam distill the dried piper nigrum fruits with 3 liters of distilled water at 100 degrees celsius for 3 hours.
Collect the distilled steam and condense it into a liquid using a condenser and a collection vessel.
Filter the liquid distillate through a 10-micron filter to remove any impurities and sediment.
Store the filtered piper nigrum essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid at room temperature.
